title: Stone Mountain

jokes.text: I had to give up my window seat to some sevenyearold who had to sit next to her precious mommy.
jokes.setup: Being asked about a trip, expecting normal travel complaints
jokes.punchline: Revealing petty selfishness toward a child wanting to sit with her mother
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Jenna's narcissistic worldview where a child's reasonable need is framed as an inconvenience to her

jokes.text: You're not going to find him in the people's gaypublic of drugifornia.
jokes.setup: Jack dismissing San Francisco as too liberal
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau combining 'gay republic' and 'drugifornia' to mock California
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that perfectly captures Jack's conservative prejudices in an economical, quotable phrase

jokes.text: Canada? Why not just go to iraq?
jokes.setup: Liz mentions Toronto auditions
jokes.punchline: Jack equating Canada with Iraq as equally foreign/hostile
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation that reveals Jack's xenophobic mindset through comedic hyperbole

jokes.text: Jack,just say Jewish. This is taking forever.
jokes.setup: Jack's long list of liberal stereotypes
jokes.punchline: Liz cutting through the euphemisms with blunt directness
jokes.explanation: Meta moment where Liz addresses both Jack's coded language and the audience's impatience with perfect economy

jokes.text: Who wants... my puddings!
jokes.setup: Someone performing what seems like physical comedy
jokes.punchline: Apparent injury during pudding-related performance
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy setup that serves plot function but lacks sophistication

jokes.text: Fred dawkins,the incredibly overweight guy that pacman was based on died last night.
jokes.setup: Announcing a death with fake backstory
jokes.punchline: Absurd claim that Pac-Man was based on a real fat person
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist joke that creates fake video game lore while setting up the episode's death theme

jokes.text: I will eat a bowl of cherries and some ghost meat in his honor.
jokes.setup: Tracy responding to Pac-Man creator's death
jokes.punchline: Honoring him by eating Pac-Man food items including 'ghost meat'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic extending the Pac-Man joke with his characteristic literal interpretation of abstract concepts

jokes.text: Orange and black decorations? Is this halloween or princeton parents' weekend? I don't know whether to be scared or proud of my cousin.
jokes.setup: Jack seeing Halloween decorations
jokes.punchline: Confusing them with Princeton colors and referencing having a Princeton relative
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Jack's elite East Coast perspective where everything relates back to Ivy League institutions

jokes.text: But at least he died doing what he loved... blogging on the huffington post.
jokes.setup: Kenneth saying famous clog dancer died doing what he loved
jokes.punchline: Subverting 'died doing what he loved' with mundane internet activity instead of clog dancing
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke that subverts the cliche about dying doing what you love with mundane modern reality

jokes.text: This is gonna be the scariest princeton parents' weekend ever!
jokes.setup: Jack still thinking about Princeton parents' weekend
jokes.punchline: Escalating the Halloween/Princeton confusion to maximum fear
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that extends the Princeton joke with escalation

jokes.text: Start over,you hack!
jokes.setup: Jenna reading someone's work
jokes.punchline: Immediately and harshly dismissing it
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate cruelty that establishes Jenna's diva behavior

jokes.text: I'm not gonna be pushed aside and forgotten, like that time at my sister's funeral.
jokes.setup: Jenna talking about not being forgotten
jokes.punchline: Comparing career fears to being upstaged at her own sister's funeral
jokes.explanation: Dark and perfectly character-specific joke showing Jenna's narcissism extends even to family tragedies

jokes.text: as her own grandma hutchins.
jokes.setup: Kenneth talking about Florence Henderson getting a great role
jokes.punchline: The 'great role' was playing her own grandmother in a nonsensical Brady Bunch storyline
jokes.explanation: Absurdist take on TV nostalgia that plays into Kenneth's confused relationship with television history

jokes.text: I don't really remember the brady bunch,'cause I was too young.
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining Brady Bunch knowledge
jokes.punchline: Claiming he was too young for a show from the 1970s despite appearing to be in his 40s
jokes.explanation: Continues Kenneth's mysterious age/background running gag with perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Although when the parcells first came to America, they lived in a town called sexcriminalboat.
jokes.setup: Kenneth being asked about his Georgia background
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning his family lived in a place with an impossibly inappropriate name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist joke that creates an impossible town name delivered with Kenneth's characteristic matter-of-fact tone

jokes.text: Two hobos sharing a bean... lady airline pilots.
jokes.setup: Asked what makes him laugh
jokes.punchline: Two completely random, mildly antiquated examples including casual sexism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment showing his old-fashioned sensibilities and genuinely weird sense of humor

jokes.text: You see,the chuckle is the part of the pig between the tail and the anus.
jokes.setup: Explaining what a 'chuckle hut' is
jokes.punchline: Creating fake pig anatomy where 'chuckle' is an actual body part
jokes.explanation: Ingenious fake etymology joke that creates absurd pig anatomy while maintaining Kenneth's earnest delivery

jokes.text: Hey,I work across the street, and I saw you guys are having a halloween party. Yeah,we are. Could you close your blinds? You're really bumming all of us out.
jokes.setup: Neighbor asking about Halloween party
jokes.punchline: Revealing the party is so sad it's depressing people in other buildings
jokes.explanation: Great escalation joke that establishes how pathetic the writers' party reputation is

jokes.text: Up is down. Black is white. Good is evil. And evil... becomes good.
jokes.setup: Frank giving motivational speech about Halloween's transformative power
jokes.punchline: Building to dramatic revelation about evil becoming good just as Jenna enters with cookies
jokes.explanation: Well-structured setup that creates perfect timing for Jenna's entrance with her manipulative niceness

jokes.text: like going bald with dignity.
jokes.setup: Jack saying the rule of threes doesn't exist
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to another impossible thing (going bald with dignity)
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor that works well as a throwaway line

jokes.text: And I don't know what this means, but he said the Island is just Hurley's dream.
jokes.setup: Writer mentioning meeting J.J. Abrams
jokes.punchline: Casually dropping fake Lost spoiler/theory with no context
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference joke that relies on Lost cultural moment but lacks deeper craft

jokes.text: Oh,wow,they painted the ceiling in here.
jokes.setup: Liz entering Southern location
jokes.punchline: Her condescending surprise at basic home maintenance
jokes.explanation: Shows Liz's unconscious snobbery through seemingly innocent observation

jokes.text: But there are not restaurants called fatty fat sandwich ranch.
jokes.setup: Jack arguing about core American values in small towns
jokes.punchline: Using comically over-the-top restaurant name as evidence
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist restaurant name that perfectly captures stereotypical Southern excess

jokes.text: which you might know better as a knish or a beignet.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining hush puppies to Liz
jokes.punchline: Condescendingly translating Southern food into ethnic equivalents
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Jack's pretentious cultural translation while being hypocritical about his own prejudices

jokes.text: All of humankind has one thing in common... the sandwich.
jokes.setup: Liz offering to share her worldview
jokes.punchline: Her profound philosophy centers entirely on sandwiches
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon moment that reduces all human philosophy to her food obsession with mock-profound delivery

jokes.text: What a surprise... your world view is foodbased.
jokes.setup: Liz's sandwich philosophy
jokes.punchline: Jack's dry acknowledgment of Liz's predictable food obsession
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that acknowledges Liz's character trait

jokes.text: Which is why I will have the carp po'boy with extra chuckle.
jokes.setup: Liz putting her sandwich philosophy into practice
jokes.punchline: Ordering something that sounds disgusting while referencing Kenneth's pig anatomy joke
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Liz's food adventurousness while referencing Kenneth's absurd pig knowledge

jokes.text: Keep refreshing. Maybe Andy dick has died in the last 20 seconds.
jokes.setup: Tracy monitoring celebrity deaths
jokes.punchline: His specific hope for Andy Dick's death
jokes.explanation: Dark but character-appropriate - Tracy's self-preservation at someone else's expense

jokes.text: Tracy! I haven't seen you since that rappinggrandma movie we did. You were so funny as the rapping grandma.
jokes.setup: Tracy calls Betty White trying to scare her into dying
jokes.punchline: She remembers him playing a rapping grandma in a movie
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd casting - Tracy as a rapping grandma is perfectly ridiculous and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You were so funny as the rapping grandma.
jokes.setup: Betty White talking to Tracy about past collaboration
jokes.punchline: Revealing Betty White played a rapping grandma in a Tracy movie
jokes.explanation: Absurd casting that creates funny mental image of Betty White in Tracy's ridiculous filmography

jokes.text: I will bury you.
jokes.setup: Betty White being suspicious of Tracy's call
jokes.punchline: Sweet Betty White making threatening declaration
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Betty White's sweet image and unexpected menacing threat

jokes.text: No,it's not because we're from new York. We're all the same sandwiches.
jokes.setup: Hotel clerk stereotypes them as New Yorkers
jokes.punchline: Liz tries to use her sandwich philosophy to defend against prejudice
jokes.explanation: Good callback where Liz desperately applies her food philosophy to real prejudice.

jokes.text: Oh,wait,they already have. It's called Manhattan. Down here we call it sexcriminalboat.
jokes.setup: Hotel clerk suggesting rounding up people like Liz on an island
jokes.punchline: Revealing Manhattan and Kenneth's ancestral town have the same name locally
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that creates fake etymology connecting Manhattan to Kenneth's absurd hometown

jokes.text: Down here we call it sexcriminalboat.
jokes.setup: Just finished Manhattan insult
jokes.punchline: References Kenneth's ridiculous ancestral town name
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that pays off Kenneth's earlier absurd town name.

jokes.text: We kiss each other,get into vans,black out.
jokes.setup: Jenna describing how crazy gay Halloween parties get
jokes.punchline: Escalating from innocent fun to potentially dangerous behavior
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that reveals Jenna's wild lifestyle through increasingly concerning activities

jokes.text: I mean,I'm engaged,but not on halloween.
jokes.setup: Jenna describing her wild Halloween behavior
jokes.punchline: Casually dismissing her engagement as irrelevant on Halloween
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna logic where Halloween temporarily suspends all moral commitments

jokes.text: God,are you doing this to me 'cause I took that blind guy's hot dog?
jokes.setup: Lutz frustrated about the situation
jokes.punchline: Revealing he previously stole food from a blind person
jokes.explanation: Dark character reveal that shows Lutz's moral failings through casual confession

jokes.text: What is this? 'peppy bismilk'?
jokes.setup: Liz receiving stomach medicine
jokes.punchline: Local knockoff brand with slightly wrong name
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about generic brands having slightly off names

jokes.text: Why is everything a little different here?
jokes.setup: Liz noticing generic brand differences
jokes.punchline: Her xenophobic complaint about minor regional variations
jokes.explanation: Shows Liz's intolerance for anything outside her narrow New York experience

jokes.text: choir member,desert storm veteran,father of three?
jokes.setup: Jack defending the hotel clerk
jokes.punchline: Adding increasingly sympathetic qualities to guilt Liz
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that builds sympathy for random character to make Liz look worse

jokes.text: Your truck don't yell at you every time you get gas.
jokes.setup: Ventriloquist routine about difference between girlfriend and truck
jokes.punchline: Truck doesn't complain when you fill it up (double meaning with money/gas)
jokes.explanation: Generic relationship humor that represents the kind of basic comedy Jack thinks will work

jokes.text: Hey,Rick,what's the difference between your girlfriend and your truck? I don't know,pumpkin. What is the difference? Your truck don't yell at you every time you get gas.
jokes.setup: Standard ventriloquist setup with Rick and his dummy
jokes.punchline: Classic 'wife nags, truck doesn't' joke structure
jokes.explanation: Deliberately generic ventriloquist humor that represents the kind of comedy Jack thinks is 'authentic' but is actually hacky.

jokes.text: Girl,I don't even have the energy to tell you what's happening here,so read my face.
jokes.setup: Someone needing to explain a complex situation
jokes.punchline: Giving up on exposition and asking audience to infer from facial expression
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about TV exposition that breaks fourth wall by acknowledging visual storytelling

jokes.text: has been hired by a catholic to appear on tgs with a black fella.
jokes.setup: Local news report about Rick Wayne being hired
jokes.punchline: Describing Jack as 'a catholic' and the show by Tracy's race
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on how small-town news would describe the show and characters

jokes.text: 'Schwupps ginny pale'?
jokes.setup: Jack bringing Liz ginger ale
jokes.punchline: Another knockoff brand with mangled name
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag of slightly wrong brand names in the South

jokes.text: like matlock... or wholesome,like elly mae clampett. And some of them are skeevy dirt bags like the dukes of hazzard, driving around like maniacs. Children use those roads.
jokes.setup: Liz defending Southerners from Jack's stereotypes
jokes.punchline: Using only TV characters as examples and getting genuinely angry about fictional reckless driving
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation where Liz's TV-based knowledge leads to genuine outrage about fictional characters' behavior

jokes.text: They named it Jack. And it was delicious.
jokes.setup: Jack talking about assisting in a foal's birth and it being named after him
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing he ate the baby horse
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark misdirection that builds up heartwarming moment before shocking subversion

jokes.text: When did you find time to eat a diaper that you found on the beach?
jokes.setup: Jack commenting on Liz's bad breath
jokes.punchline: Incredibly specific and gross scenario for how she got that taste
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and disgusting insult that creates vivid mental image

jokes.text: Pete hornberger Alan Parsons project project.
jokes.setup: Liz mentioning Pete's cover band
jokes.punchline: Band name that adds 'project' to 'Alan Parsons Project' creating redundant 'project project'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay joke about redundant cover band naming that creates musical absurdity

jokes.text: Get it? I'm an Italian senator.
jokes.setup: Jenna showing off her Halloween costume
jokes.punchline: Costume joke playing on Italian political corruption stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Moderate costume joke that relies on stereotype without much sophistication

jokes.text: Are we cowabunga on this?
jokes.setup: Jenna making demands for her cooperation
jokes.punchline: Using 'cowabunga' as business slang confirmation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna malapropism mixing outdated slang with business negotiation

jokes.text: I don't go down to where you work and stop them from milking you.
jokes.setup: Ventriloquist dummy responding to Liz's heckling
jokes.punchline: Implying Liz works as livestock being milked
jokes.explanation: Strong comeback that escalates the insult battle with crude but effective imagery

jokes.text: Well,I'm an amateur astronomer,and Rick's black wife speaks French.
jokes.setup: Pumpkin defending against accusations of being simple
jokes.punchline: Two completely random facts that don't prove intelligence
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection where the 'proof' of sophistication is completely random and irrelevant

jokes.text: Do you ever find smaller heads orbiting your head?
jokes.setup: Pumpkin insulting Liz's head size
jokes.punchline: Suggesting her head is so large it has gravitational pull
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of insult that uses physics/astronomy humor for creative put-down

jokes.text: I can't. I got a man's arm stuck up me. But you know what that's like.
jokes.setup: Pumpkin being challenged to come down and fight
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging he's a puppet then implying Liz also has someone's arm inside her
jokes.explanation: Exceptional meta joke where ventriloquist dummy acknowledges its nature while delivering crushing sexual insult

jokes.text: Oh,God,he's murdering me. His head is so much bigger up close!
jokes.setup: Physical altercation begins between Jack and Rick Wayne
jokes.punchline: Pumpkin continues the head size insults even during the fight
jokes.explanation: Good callback that maintains the head insult theme even during physical comedy.

tmdb_overview: Jack and Liz search for new talent as they travel to Kenneth's hometown. Jenna makes an attempt to become friends with the writers. Tracy fears he may die next after a pair of celebrities pass away.
insight: Absurdist escalation drives 56 jokes in 38 minutes—30 Rock's densest fourth-season output.
